title: USD Strength Outlook Q2 2026
summary: Analysis of USD momentum heading into Q2 driven by Fed policy expectations.
content: The US dollar has shown resilience amid shifting rate expectations. Key drivers include persistent inflation data and labor market tightness. We expect the DXY to trade in the 104-107 range through Q2.
author: Sarah Mitchell

title: EUR/USD Technical Levels to Watch
summary: Key support and resistance zones for EUR/USD in the near term.
content: EUR/USD is consolidating near the 1.0820 level after failing to break above 1.0900. The 200-day moving average at 1.0780 provides critical support. A break below opens the door to 1.0700.
author: Marco Rossi

title: GBP Impact from UK Fiscal Policy
summary: How upcoming UK budget decisions may influence the British pound.
content: The UK government faces challenging fiscal choices that could weigh on sterling. Potential tax increases and spending cuts may slow growth, while the Bank of England remains cautious on rate cuts.
author: Emily Carter

title: JPY Carry Trade Dynamics in 2026
summary: Examining whether the yen carry trade remains attractive given BOJ policy shifts.
content: With the BOJ gradually normalizing policy, the traditional yen carry trade faces headwinds. However, the wide rate differential with the US continues to support short-yen positions for now.
author: Kenji Tanaka

title: AUD/NZD Cross Rate Opportunity
summary: Relative value opportunity in the Antipodean cross as fundamentals diverge.
content: Australian economic data has outperformed New Zealand recently, creating a tactical long AUD/NZD opportunity. We target 1.1050 with a stop at 1.0850.
author: Liam O'Brien

title: CHF Safe Haven Flows Monitor
summary: Tracking safe haven demand for the Swiss franc amid geopolitical uncertainty.
content: Geopolitical tensions have periodically boosted CHF demand. We monitor EUR/CHF positioning and SNB intervention signals as key indicators for franc direction.
author: Sarah Mitchell

title: Emerging Market FX Weekly Review
summary: Weekly performance review of major EM currencies against the dollar.
content: EM currencies had a mixed week. BRL and MXN outperformed on commodity strength, while TRY and ZAR lagged due to domestic policy concerns. Overall EM sentiment remains cautiously optimistic.
author: Hugo Santos

title: CAD and Oil Price Correlation Update
summary: Reassessing the CAD-crude oil relationship as energy markets evolve.
content: The historical correlation between CAD and WTI oil prices has weakened in 2026. Diversification of Canada's economy and shifting trade patterns suggest a more nuanced approach to trading USD/CAD.
author: Emily Carter

title: Central Bank Watch: April 2026
summary: Preview of upcoming central bank meetings and expected policy decisions.
content: April features decisions from the ECB, BOC, and RBA. Markets price a 60% chance of an ECB cut, while the BOC and RBA are expected to hold. We outline scenario analysis for each.
author: Marco Rossi

title: USD/CNH: Navigating Trade Policy Risks
summary: How evolving trade policies affect the offshore yuan outlook.
content: Trade policy uncertainty continues to create volatility in USD/CNH. We analyze tariff scenarios and PBOC management of the daily fix as key drivers for the pair.
author: Kenji Tanaka

title: Volatility Smile Analysis: Major Pairs
summary: Options market signals for G10 currency pairs.
content: Risk reversals across major pairs show elevated demand for USD calls vs EUR and GBP puts, signaling hedging activity against further dollar strength. Vol surfaces suggest limited downside risk to USD.
author: Sarah Mitchell

title: NOK/SEK Divergence Play
summary: Scandinavian currencies offer a mean-reversion opportunity.
content: NOK has underperformed SEK by 3% YTD despite similar macro fundamentals. Oil price stabilization and Norges Bank rhetoric support a tactical long NOK/SEK position targeting 0.9850.
author: Erik Svensson

title: Digital Currency Impact on FX Markets
summary: How CBDCs and stablecoin adoption are reshaping foreign exchange flows.
content: Central bank digital currencies are progressing from pilot to implementation in several economies. We examine how this may affect cross-border payment flows and traditional FX market structure.
author: Liam O'Brien

title: GBP/JPY Breakout Watch
summary: GBP/JPY approaching a multi-month range breakout level.
content: GBP/JPY has been compressing in a 188.00-192.50 range for eight weeks. Momentum indicators suggest a breakout is imminent. A close above 192.50 targets 196.00; below 188.00 opens 184.50.
author: Marco Rossi

title: Quarterly FX Forecast Update
summary: Updated forecasts for major and select EM currency pairs through Q4 2026.
content: We revise our EUR/USD forecast lower to 1.0600 by year-end from 1.0900 previously. GBP/USD is adjusted to 1.2400. USD/JPY target raised to 158. Full forecast table included.
author: Emily Carter

title: EUR/USD Bulls Target 1.1000 as ECB Signals Pause
summary: The euro has surged to multi-month highs against the dollar after ECB officials signalled a prolonged pause in their rate-cut cycle. Technical breakout above 1.0900 opens the path to 1.1000.
content:
The EUR/USD pair extended its rally for a third consecutive session on Friday, climbing to 1.0965 as European Central Bank policymakers reinforced their data-dependent stance and pushed back against aggressive easing expectations.

Key Drivers:

  • ECB Governing Council member Schnabel warned that inflation stickiness in services could delay further cuts.
  • US non-farm payrolls missed estimates at +165k vs +200k expected, weakening dollar demand.
  • Euro-zone PMI data surprised to the upside, with the composite index returning to expansion at 50.4.

Technical Outlook:

Having broken above the 200-day moving average at 1.0855, bulls are now targeting the psychological 1.1000 level. Immediate support sits at 1.0900, while a close below 1.0820 would invalidate the bullish bias. The RSI at 62 leaves room for further upside before overbought territory.

Trading Recommendation: Long EUR/USD on dips towards 1.0900 with a target of 1.1050 and stop loss below 1.0820.
author: Elena Marchetti

title: GBP/USD Retreats as UK Inflation Undershoots Forecasts
summary: Sterling pulled back sharply from 1.2850 after UK CPI printed below consensus, raising expectations of an early Bank of England rate cut. Downside risk towards 1.2600 increases.
content:
The British pound fell over 0.8% against the US dollar on Wednesday after official data showed UK consumer price inflation eased more than anticipated to 2.8% year-on-year in February, down from 3.0% in January and below the 2.9% market consensus.

Key Drivers:

  • UK CPI fell to 2.8% YoY, core CPI dropped to 3.5% from 3.7%.
  • Money markets now price a 70% probability of a May BoE rate cut, up from 45% pre-data.
  • Dollar remained well-bid after Fed Chair Powell reiterated patience on rate cuts.

Technical Outlook:

GBP/USD broke below the short-term uptrend support at 1.2800. Next key support lies at the 50-day moving average near 1.2700. A sustained break below 1.2700 would open the way towards 1.2600. Resistance is now seen at 1.2850.

Trading Recommendation: Short GBP/USD on recoveries towards 1.2800 targeting 1.2650, stop above 1.2870.
author: James Whitmore

title: USD/JPY Consolidates Near 150.00 Ahead of BoJ Decision
summary: The yen is treading water near the critical 150.00 level as traders await the Bank of Japan's policy announcement. A hawkish surprise could trigger sharp yen appreciation.
content:
USD/JPY is consolidating in a narrow 149.50-150.50 range as market participants cautiously position ahead of the Bank of Japan's policy meeting scheduled for next week.

Key Drivers:

  • BoJ Governor Ueda hinted at further normalisation but stopped short of signalling an imminent hike.
  • Japan's wage growth data for February showed a 3.2% increase, supporting the hawkish narrative.
  • US Treasury yields firmed, providing underlying support for USD/JPY.

Technical Outlook:

The pair is forming a symmetrical triangle pattern. A break above 150.80 could trigger a move to 152.00, while a break below 149.20 would open the path to 148.00.

Trading Recommendation: Neutral - wait for BoJ resolution before initiating new positions.
author: Kenji Tanaka

title: AUD/USD: China Stimulus Hopes Lift Australian Dollar
summary: The Aussie rallied to 0.6580 as Beijing announced a broad fiscal stimulus package, boosting risk appetite and commodity demand. Further upside eyed toward 0.6650.
content:
AUD/USD advanced 0.6% to 0.6580 on Thursday, its highest level since January, after China's State Council unveiled a CNY 2 trillion fiscal stimulus package targeting infrastructure and domestic consumption.

Key Drivers:

  • China's CNY 2 trillion stimulus package boosted risk sentiment globally.
  • Iron ore prices rose 3.5% to $115/tonne, supporting the Australian dollar.
  • RBA minutes struck a balanced tone, not pre-committing to further cuts.

Technical Outlook:

The break above the 0.6550 resistance zone is constructive. The next resistance is at 0.6620, followed by 0.6650. Support is at 0.6520.

Trading Recommendation: Long AUD/USD on pullbacks to 0.6540 targeting 0.6650, stop below 0.6490.
author: Sarah Lin

title: Technical Analysis: Dollar Index Approaching Critical Resistance
summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) is testing major resistance at 105.00. A decisive rejection could herald a broader dollar downtrend, reshaping FX landscape heading into Q2 2026.
content:
The US Dollar Index (DXY) is at a crossroads. After a 3% rally from early-February lows, the index is testing the 105.00 level.

Technical Picture:

  • Resistance: 105.00 (key level), 105.60 (200-week MA)
  • Support: 103.80 (50-day MA), 103.00 (trend support)
  • RSI (14): 58 - approaching but not yet overbought
  • MACD: Positive crossover in place but momentum slowing

Scenario Analysis:

Bullish Dollar Scenario (probability 35%): A clean break above 105.00 opens the path to 106.50.

Bearish Dollar Scenario (probability 65%): Rejection at 105.00 and reversal below 104.50 would signal the end of the corrective rally.
author: Marcus Reeves

title: Market Outlook: Fed Pivot Timeline Shifts - What It Means for FX
summary: Stronger US data has pushed the first Fed rate cut expectation back to September 2026. We examine how this shift reshapes the FX landscape across major pairs and EM currencies.
content:
The repricing of Fed expectations following a string of upside US economic surprises has materially shifted the first cut expectation from June to September 2026.

Impact by Currency Pair:

  • EUR/USD: Rate differential compression trade is delayed. Upside capped near 1.1000.
  • GBP/USD: BoE likely to cut before Fed now. Sterling faces additional headwinds.
  • USD/JPY: Elevated US rates keep upside pressure, but BoJ normalisation should cap gains at 153.00.
  • AUD/USD: Chinese stimulus provides an offsetting positive.
  • EM Currencies: Delayed Fed cuts are negative for EM.
author: Dr. Alexandra Ross

title: USD/CHF: Safe-Haven Demand Supports Franc Amid Geopolitical Tensions
summary: Renewed geopolitical uncertainty in Eastern Europe has driven safe-haven flows into the Swiss franc. USD/CHF dipped below parity - is it heading toward 0.9800?
content:
The Swiss franc strengthened across the board on Monday as geopolitical tensions flared in Eastern Europe, driving traditional safe-haven demand into CHF and JPY. USD/CHF fell below parity to trade at 0.9930.

Key Drivers:

  • Geopolitical risk premium returned after ceasefire negotiations stalled.
  • SNB reiterated willingness to intervene in FX market if needed.
  • EUR/CHF fell to 1.0750, approaching SNB comfort floor near 1.0650.

Technical Outlook:

USD/CHF has broken below the ascending trend channel. Next support is at 0.9880 (2025 low), followed by 0.9800.

Trading Recommendation: Cautious short USD/CHF below 0.9980 targeting 0.9880. Stop loss at 1.0050.
author: Hans Weber

title: Fundamental Analysis: Why EUR/USD Could Break Above 1.1200 in Q2 2026
summary: A confluence of fundamental tailwinds - ECB pause, US fiscal concerns, and euro-zone recovery - presents a structural case for EUR/USD to reclaim levels not seen since early 2024.
content:
Looking beyond near-term noise, a structural case is building for a sustained EUR/USD rally toward 1.1200 by the end of Q2 2026.

1. ECB Rate Pause Creates Relative Yield Support

With the ECB signalling a pause after four 25bp cuts, the EUR-USD 2-year swap spread has narrowed significantly.

2. US Fiscal Concerns Weigh on Long-Term Dollar Outlook

The US Congressional Budget Office projects the federal deficit to remain above 5% of GDP through 2028.

3. Euro-zone Cyclical Recovery Underway

After three quarters of stagnation, euro-zone GDP grew 0.4% in Q4 2025.

12-Month Target: EUR/USD 1.1200. Build long positions on dips below 1.0900.
author: Elena Marchetti

title: GBP/JPY Weekly Technical Note
summary: Preliminary technical analysis of GBP/JPY ahead of the upcoming BoJ and BoE decisions. This note is pending final review before publication.
content: This is a draft research note in progress...
author: James Whitmore
